business_hours: Monday to Friday, 8:00 AM to 4:30 PM
emergency_definition: After-hours calls from GNM Pressure Washing clients (Chevron/Esso gas stations) when gas stations are running 24/7
emergency_routing_rules: After-hours emergency calls from GNM Pressure Washing clients will be patched directly to Ben's second (personal) phone number. Clara will gather details such as customer name, property name, address, and primary contact before patching.
non_emergency_routing_rules: During business hours: Initially, if Ben does not answer or declines a call on his main business line, it will be forwarded to Clara. Once Ben's second number is active, the main business line will be forwarded directly to Clara as the first point of contact. Clara will handle new clients, small jobs, and general service calls. If a caller explicitly requests to speak with Ben, Clara will determine the purpose of the call and transfer it to Ben's second number. After hours (for all other callers except GNM emergencies): Clara will inform callers that they will receive a callback on the next business day.
call_transfer_rules: If Ben does not answer or declines a call on his main business line, the call will be forwarded to Clara. If a caller requests to speak with Ben, Clara will gather the purpose of the call and then transfer the call to Ben's second (personal) number. What to say if transfer fails is not specified.
after_hours_flow_summary: No general emergency calls are handled after hours. For one specific client, GNM Pressure Washing (a property management company managing Chevron/Esso gas stations), after-hours emergencies related to 24/7 operating gas stations will be patched through directly to Ben's second (personal) number. For all other callers after hours, Clara will inform them that they will receive a callback on the next business day.
office_hours_flow_summary: During business hours, the main business line will forward calls to Clara. Initially, Clara will answer if Ben doesn't or declines. Once Ben's dedicated second (personal) number is set up, Clara will become the primary point of contact for all incoming calls. Clara will handle new clients, small jobs, and general service calls. If a caller explicitly requests to speak with Ben, Clara will determine the call's purpose and then transfer it to his second number. Clara will mention the service call fee ($115 call-out fee, plus $98/hour for residential in half-hour increments after the first half-hour) only if specifically asked by the customer.
questions_or_unknowns: What is the specific local timezone for the business hours (8:00 AM - 4:30 PM)?
What is Ben's main business line number?
What is Ben's second (personal) number for direct transfers?
What happens if a call transfer to Ben's second number fails?
What is the physical office address?
Is there a general emergency definition for clients other than GNM Pressure Washing?
notes: Initial setup will involve Clara answering calls if Ben's main business line is not answered or declined. A second phase will activate Clara as the primary call handler for the main line, utilizing a new, dedicated personal number for Ben to receive transferred calls. Clara should only mention the pricing structure ($115 call-out fee + $98/hour for residential, billed in half-hour increments after the first half-hour) when specifically asked by a customer. A follow-up call is scheduled for Friday at 2:00 PM to review testing calls and the dashboard. Ben uses an Android device for call forwarding setup.
